name: Oscar Moore
abstract: Oscar Frederic Moore (December 25, 1916 – October 8, 1981) was an American jazz guitarist with the Nat King Cole Trio.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: Oscar Moore for those beautiful chords. And then there was a couple of other things that I should mention... That also was a big influence on me early on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Clarence Ford
about_quotes.evidence: Lloyd played bass, Oscar played drums. Oscar Moore. Yeah. He was with Slam before any of them... And I played baritone.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Nat formed a trio with Wesley Prince... and Oscar Moore... we used to go by and share pots of ham hocks and beans

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: It’s the 1946 Esquire All- American band... Oscar Moore, guitar... Chubby Jackson, string bass
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Cotton
about_quotes.evidence: we had Oscar Moore played the drums Lloyd played the bass and I played the piano

about_quotes.speaker: Leon Rene
about_quotes.evidence: he got a little trio of uh Oscar Moore and I can't think of their bass man's player Prince Wesley Prince yeah Wesley Prince and so they they got so they went on 8th street and got a little job in a nightclub and that's where they went I found them there and made the first record of Nat King Cole
